mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0% within a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous surges in industrial production and declines in housing starts signal supply-side bottlenecks and inflationary pressure, forcing higher discount rates and compressing equity valuations, which yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample windows, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, stable consumer price inflation combined with persistently low federal funds rates validates the earnings recovery narrative, sustaining positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns turn negative in more than 50% of observations where CPIAUCSL growth remains below 2.5% and FEDFUNDS stays under 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous month-over-month growth in real personal savings and consumption expenditures directly fuels corporate top-line growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days without requiring regime filters.
failure_condition: The rolling 3-month Spearman correlation between RSAFS momentum and 5-day SPX returns never exceeds 0.15 across the full sample period.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index rises by more than 1.0 percent over a single month, it confirms robust manufacturing capacity utilization and profit margin expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are flat or negative in more than 60% of months where INDPRO exceeds the 1.0% monthly growth threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate validate a self-reinforcing labor demand cycle, which sustains consumer spending and equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fails to explain more than 5% of the variance in 60-day SPX returns, or out-of-sample directional accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion, diverging housing starts and slowing GDP growth signal demand exhaustion and margin compression, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings revisions turn sharply negative.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 3% while GDPC1 growth drops below 2.0% annualized
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 65% of triggered instances, or the forward return differential between triggered and non-triggered periods is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).
